reasoning: As of November 9, 2025, Elon Musk remains the CEO of Tesla. Recent news from early November 2025 indicates that Tesla shareholders just approved a massive new pay package for Musk (or re-approved a prior one), affirming his continued leadership. There are only about 50 days left in the year. The base rate for an unexpected, immediate CEO departure in a 1.5 month window is extremely low (typically around 1-2% for average CEOs, and even lower for highly entrenched founder-CEOs like Musk without a pre-announced transition plan). In the absence of any credible reports predicting an imminent step-down or a transition arrangement by the end of 2025, the probability that he will cease to be CEO in the remaining weeks of the year is at the minimum threshold of 2%.
